etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "speech") + phonetic 亥(亥) (hài)
etymology.interpretation: The character 該 combines 言(言) (yán, "speech") as the semantic element, representing verbal communication or stating something, with 亥(亥) (hài) as the phonetic element to provide the sound. It suggests the context of what is spoken or stated, leading to the meaning "ought to" or "should", as in something that is verbally recommended or prescribed.
etymology.simplification: 該(該) was simplified to 该(該) by replacing 言(言) (yán, "speech") with 讠, the simplified radical form for speech components.

examples.chinese: 你该早点休息(你該早点休息)。
我们该去超市了(我們該去超市了)。
examples.english: We should go to the supermarket.

examples.chinese: 他认为你该听从建议(他認為你該聽從建議)。
examples.pinyin: Tā rènwéi nǐ gāi tīngcóng jiànyì.
examples.english: He thinks you ought to listen to advice.
